ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'tea beverage' class (FOODON_03301099); no specific jasmine green tea with tapioca pearls class exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers green tea as a beverage category. No specific jasmine-flavoured or pearl-added subcode exists; closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for jasmine green tea with tapioca pearls identified in the clinical terminology. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving is approximately 350 g (one medium takeaway cup), which includes brewed jasmine green tea, sugar syrup, and a portion of cooked tapioca pearls.

nutrient_source.title: Jasmine Green Tea with Pearls — HPB Singapore Foodo Database (CR ID D08010062)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record D08010062 for Jasmine green tea with pearls. Sodium was reported as -1 (data not available) in the source record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for jasmine green tea with tapioca pearls as a combined beverage. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on: (1) tapioca pearls GI ~60–70 per University of Sydney GI Database analogous starch entries; (2) brewed green tea contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter GI; (3) added sugar (sucrose) GI ~65. Weighted composite estimate of ~60 is defensible. Glycemic load per 350 g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 25.7) / 100 ≈ 15.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with added sugar syrup; sodium data unavailable but beverage is not a significant sodium source
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit sugar-sweetened beverages including jasmine green tea with pearls, as regular SSB consumption is independently associated with elevated blood pressure and metabolic syndrome; request an unsweetened version without pearls.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI tapioca pearls combined with added sugar syrup produce rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g) provides no glycaemic buffering
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid jasmine green tea with tapioca pearls due to its medium-to-high glycaemic index, added sugar content, and near-zero dietary fibre, all of which contribute to rapid blood glucose spikes; plain unsweetened jasmine green tea is a suitable alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with increased triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and cardiovascular risk; fat and saturated fat are negligible
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit intake of this sugar-sweetened beverage, as habitual SSB consumption raises triglycerides and promotes weight gain; the absence of fat and sodium is not sufficient to classify this drink as heart-healthy given its added sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jasmine Green Tea with Tapioca Pearls is a popular sweetened cold beverage widely sold at bubble tea and speciality tea stalls across Singapore and Southeast Asia. It is prepared by brewing jasmine-scented green tea leaves or jasmine green tea bags, chilling the brew, adding sugar syrup or simple syrup to sweeten, and serving over ice with cooked black tapioca pearls (boba) that have been simmered in sugar solution until soft and chewy. The drink has a floral, lightly astringent green tea flavour balanced by sweetness, with the tapioca pearls providing a dense, chewy textural contrast. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 29 kcal, 7.35 g carbohydrate (of which 4.45 g is sugar), 0.03 g dietary fibre, and negligible protein and fat. Protein and fat are confirmed zero by the HPB source formulation — this is a carbohydrate-dominant beverage with energy derived almost entirely from added sugar and tapioca starch. Clinically, this drink is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its added sugar content, high-GI tapioca pearls, and near-zero fibre. The jasmine green tea base contains trace polyphenols including EGCG, but the sweetened, pearl-added preparation negates any meaningful anti-inflammatory or metabolic benefit. An unsweetened jasmine green tea without pearls is the recommended alternative for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record D08010062 for this exact food item. Source match type is exact. Confidence is high for all reported macros and micronutrients. Sodium was reported as -1 in the HPB source, indicating the value was not available in the original dataset.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo record D08010062 — this is a tea-based beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients. fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo record D08010062 — no fat-containing ingredients are present in the standard preparation. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by formulation. sodium_mg set to -1: sodium data was not reported in the HPB source record (input value was -1.0); detailed sodium data for this specific preparation is not available in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and fatty acid values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this record and are not available in other approved sources for this specific preparation. GI estimated at 60 (medium) using analogous tapioca starch and sucrose entries from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this combined beverage exists in approved sources. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (60 × 25.7) / 100 ≈ 15.4 g. This food is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under Singapore HPB dietary guidelines.
